description: Default semiconductor RCA knowledge pack. Setup copies this into flow-data only when missing; runtime can also read this code seed directly.

knowledge_cards.title: GAA short-Lg DIBL and SS increase
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Worse gate electrostatic control increases drain coupling, subthreshold slope, and short-channel Vth roll-off.
structural_causes: gate stack EOT or work-function variation
supporting_evidence: DIBL and SS increase together on short Lg structures
Vth roll-off worsens with Lg scaling
Inline gate/channel CD or spacer metric shifts in same lots
contradicting_evidence: Only long-Lg Vth shifts without DIBL/SS change
No matching Inline/gate CD or spacer shift
Wafer-local pattern suggests defectivity instead of global electrostatics
recommended_checks: Plot DIBL vs SS by Lg and polarity
Correlate short-Lg DIBL with gate/channel CD
Check Vth roll-off trend vs route split
Review channel release and inner spacer Inline modules
chart_suggestions: DIBL vs SS scatter with Lg color
wafer map of DIBL and SS

knowledge_cards.title: Ion decrease with Rsd increase
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Higher source/drain or access resistance reduces drive current even when gate electrostatics is not the primary limiter.
structural_causes: S/D epi volume or dopant activation loss
supporting_evidence: Ion decreases while Rsd/RS increases
Vth and DIBL are relatively stable
CA_RC or chain resistance also increases
contradicting_evidence: Ion decrease only at high leakage or Vth shift
Rsd unchanged
Only one wafer edge affected with defect signature
missing_data: Rsd split by n/p and geometry
recommended_checks: Check S/D epi Inline by lot_wf

knowledge_cards.title: CA resistance increase with CA CD decrease
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Smaller contact opening or degraded contact interface increases contact/chain resistance.
process_root_causes: metal fill or barrier process drift
supporting_evidence: CA_CD decreases while CA_RC_KELVIN or chain resistance increases
Contact chain fail/open increases
Affected lots share CA/MOL route or chamber
contradicting_evidence: CA_RS name is sheet-resistance structure rather than Kelvin/contact
CA_CD stable and resistance only in one device polarity
Resistance shift follows S/D epi not contact module
recommended_checks: Resolve CA_RS using unit/test_structure before RCA
Check chain open rate by wafer map

knowledge_cards.title: Global Vth shift
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Gate work-function, EOT, fixed charge, or channel doping changes move threshold voltage globally.
supporting_evidence: Vth shifts on both short and long Lg without strong DIBL/SS degradation
Ion/Ioff move consistently with Vth direction
RMG/WFM monitor or route split aligns
contradicting_evidence: Only short-Lg affected with DIBL/SS increase
Only contact resistance metrics shift
strong die-local spatial defect pattern
recommended_checks: Compare Vth shift with EOT/WFM monitor

knowledge_cards.title: Gate leakage increase
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Gate dielectric thinning, trap generation, or local defects increase tunneling or trap-assisted leakage.
process_root_causes: RMG thermal or metal contamination issue
supporting_evidence: IGATE increases with EOT/thickness monitor shift
leakage map has module-specific spatial signature
SS or Vth instability accompanies leakage
contradicting_evidence: Only drain leakage/IOFF changes with DIBL
No gate bias dependence
contact resistance metrics dominate Ion loss
recommended_checks: Plot IGATE by gate bias and device size
Review RMG and gate dielectric chambers

knowledge_cards.title: SRAM Vmin increase with variability
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Vmin is sensitive to transistor strength mismatch, leakage, Vth sigma, and local defect/fail signatures.
structural_causes: Vth sigma or leakage tail increase
supporting_evidence: SRAM Vmin tail increases with Vth sigma or Ion sigma
macro/condition-specific fail signature
EDS/VM wafer map spatially matches Inline/ET variation
contradicting_evidence: global Vth shift only without sigma/tail increase
VM fail isolated to test condition issue
no ET/Inline proxy movement
recommended_checks: Compare VM fail map with ET/Inline wafer maps
Separate global shift from tail/sigma issue

knowledge_cards.title: QTIME or queue exposure linked electrical shift
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Time-between-step exposure can change surface condition, oxide/regrowth, residue, or contamination before a sensitive module.
supporting_evidence: metric shift correlates with from_step/to_step duration before sensitive module
effect appears after QTIME window and before measurement
same chamber not sufficient without time window
contradicting_evidence: QTIME window occurs after measurement
no duration separation between good/bad lots
spatial signature points to local defectivity
recommended_checks: Check p95 and max duration not only median

knowledge_cards.title: EDS edge or reticle-local fail pattern
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Die-level yield or fail pattern often separates global process shift from spatial defectivity, edge effect, or reticle field issue.
structural_causes: CMP or film thickness spatial variation
supporting_evidence: fail bins cluster by die_x/die_y or reticle field
ET/Inline wafer map matches spatial pattern
global lot median is less sensitive than tail/fail-rate
contradicting_evidence: all die shift uniformly by lot
no spatial clustering
test condition artifact explains fail bin
recommended_checks: Plot fail-rate by wafer and reticle field
Compare EDS map with Inline/ET map

knowledge_cards.title: Inline CD shift linked to ET metric
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Geometry and CD changes can modulate electrostatics, contact area, and drive current depending on layer and structure.
supporting_evidence: Inline metric and ET metric correlate by lot_wf or shot
effect direction matches physical mechanism
same module timing precedes ET measurement
contradicting_evidence: correlation disappears after lot/wafer grouping
Inline measured after ET or unrelated layer
single outlier drives fit
recommended_checks: Use shot key when both sides have coordinates
INLINE avg and ET median by lot_wf if no exact match
chart_suggestions: Inline CD vs ET scatter with linear fit

knowledge_cards.title: Resistance decomposition: Rch, Rs/Rd, Rc
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Total drive degradation can come from channel resistance, source/drain access resistance, or contact resistance; metrics must be decomposed before assigning process cause.
supporting_evidence: Rch moves without CA_RC shift for channel hypothesis
CA_RC_KELVIN or chain moves with Rc hypothesis
Rsd moves with S/D epi or activation evidence
contradicting_evidence: Using CA_RS raw name without structure
Ion shift explained by Vth/leakage instead of resistance
mixed device polarity or geometry
missing_data: test structure for each resistance metric
recommended_checks: Check Kelvin vs chain vs sheet structures

causal_edges.evidence: Channel release geometry affects electrostatic control.
Spacer asymmetry and parasitic geometry affect short-channel behavior.
EOT or dielectric damage increases gate leakage.
Epi volume and activation affect source/drain resistance.
Smaller contact CD can increase resistance.
Delay before clean/contact can change interface condition.
Local defects often appear as VM/EDS tails.
Die-level bins help verify spatial fail signatures.

historical_cases.title: Short-Lg DIBL/SS excursion traced to channel release and spacer window
evidence: DIBL and SS increased only on short-Lg ET
Inline channel/spacer proxy shifted by lot_wf
long-Lg Vth did not explain the excursion
historical_cases.resolution: Review channel release and inner spacer process window before concluding WFM root cause.

historical_cases.title: CA contact resistance tail followed CA CD shrink
evidence: Kelvin and chain structures both moved
sheet resistance structure did not move
historical_cases.resolution: Separate sheet/contact structures; review CA lithography/etch and preclean.

historical_cases.title: SRAM Vmin tail matched Vth sigma and reticle-local EDS fail
evidence: Vmin median mildly shifted but tail increased
EDS fail map clustered by reticle field
historical_cases.resolution: Use sigma/map evidence; do not conclude global WFM shift from Vmin median alone.

historical_cases.title: Contact resistance increased after clean-to-deposition queue delay
evidence: same chamber alone did not separate lots
historical_cases.resolution: Confirm time window ordering and p95/max duration before assigning QTIME root cause.
